_risk_level_doc: Changes affect session resurrection prevention and memory OOM protection — both are safety-critical in production. Incorrect tombstone timing could permanently tombstone live sessions; incorrect snapshot writes could corrupt session_dirs.txt.

requirement.original: Fix three verified structural gaps in /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh: GAP 1 — Tombstone timing window (HIGH severity); GAP 2 — Webhook-to-session_dirs.txt delay (MEDIUM severity); GAP 3 — MemoryMax defaults are infinity (MEDIUM severity).
requirement.clarified: Same as original — all three gaps are verified structural defects with clear root causes and unambiguous fix directions.
requirement.what: Three targeted fixes to /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh: (1) write tombstones at first loss detection, not after 5-minute stability; (2) call write_home_snapshot() immediately after daemon tracking confirmation in restore path; (3) change per-session memory defaults from infinity to 4G/3G/2G
requirement.why: Prevents manually-killed sessions from resurrecting after a daemon crash, ensures freshly-recovered sessions survive immediate daemon crashes, and establishes baseline OOM protection for recovery-spawned sessions

included: compute_and_record_diff() or watch loop removed-set extraction (GAP 1 support)

success_criteria: Early tombstone written at first loss detection before 5-minute window
Tombstone prevents resurrection during daemon crash in 5-minute window
session_dirs.txt updated immediately after each successful tracking confirmation
Per-session systemd units spawn with MemoryMax=4G / MemoryHigh=3G / MemorySwapMax=2G by default
Operator env-var override (HAPPY_RECOVERY_MEMORY_MAX etc.) still respected
constraints: Do NOT run happy-session-recovery.sh directly (bash safety hook permanently blocks it)
Atomic write pattern (tmp+mv) must be preserved in any snapshot write calls
Changes must not break the existing 5-minute stability window behavior — tombstones are additive, not a replacement for the stability gate on session_dirs.txt

root_cause_analysis.symptom: Three structural timing gaps in the recovery script allow (1) manually-killed sessions to resurrect after a daemon crash, (2) recovered sessions to be lost if the daemon crashes within 60 seconds of recovery, and (3) OOM runaway sessions to exhaust all server memory
root_cause_analysis.root_cause: GAP 1: tombstone writing is coupled to the stability acceptance path (5-minute window), not to loss detection. GAP 2: restore_online_sessions() does not flush session_dirs.txt after each confirmed recovery — it relies entirely on the next watcher poll. GAP 3: the comment at line 1415-1416 ('legitimately runs Happy workloads above 60G total') conflates whole-server aggregate usage with per-session limits, leading to infinity defaults.
root_cause_analysis.root_cause_commit: N/A — no bisect triggered; these are design gaps, not regressions from a specific commit
root_cause_analysis.why_introduced: GAP 1: tombstone path was designed with the stability window as the sole signal for 'user-intentional' removals, missing the race with daemon crashes. GAP 2: restore_online_sessions() was designed to delegate snapshot management to the watcher, without accounting for the window between tracking confirmation and next poll. GAP 3: per-session memory limits were intentionally left at infinity to accommodate large workloads, but no default upper bound was set.
root_cause_analysis.why_problematic: GAP 1: daemon crashes are uncorrelated with the 5-minute window; any crash in that window defeats tombstone protection. GAP 2: the watcher's poll interval creates a systematic data loss window for freshly-recovered sessions. GAP 3: a single malfunction session can exhaust 72GB (32GB RAM + 40GB swap) before the kernel OOM killer acts.
root_cause_analysis.timeline: All three gaps are present in the current version (2441-line script, last touched in recent dev cycles per git log)
observations: record_stable_loss_tombstones() called only at line 792, guarded by loss_stable_count >= 30 (inferred: code read at lines 749, 786-797)
loss_observe_mode transitions to 1 at line 833 — no tombstone write in that branch (inferred: code read at lines 831-841)
daemon_spawn_session() returns 0 at line 1478 after tracking confirmed — no write_home_snapshot() call in restore_online_sessions() caller (inferred: code read at lines 1760-1765)
Memory defaults at lines 1418-1420: recovery_memory_max=infinity, recovery_memory_high=infinity, recovery_memory_swap_max=infinity (inferred: code read)
Server memory: MemTotal 32088160 kB (~30.6 GiB), SwapTotal 41943032 kB (~40 GiB) (observed: /proc/meminfo read in this session)
root_cause_analysis.diagnosis_completeness: incomplete (inferred only — cannot run the script live due to bash safety hook; runtime behavior not directly observed)
root_cause_analysis.localization_evidence: Direct code read of /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh (2441 lines) in this session. All three gaps traced to exact line numbers.

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: manually-killed session gets resurrected on next boot
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Tombstone written at first loss detection (loss_observe_mode transitions to 1), before 5-minute stability window elapses

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: recovered session is not persisted and will not be restored on next boot
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: write_home_snapshot() called for the home after each successful daemon_spawn_session() + wait_for_daemon_tracking() pair

requirements_decomposition.source_phrase: OOM protection is never enforced by default
requirements_decomposition.acceptance_criterion: Lines 1418-1420 default values changed from infinity to 4G / 3G / 2G; env-var override preserved

component_chain.role: watch_sessions() — main watcher loop; detects loss, manages loss_observe_mode, calls record_stable_loss_tombstones() only after 5-minute stability
component_chain.evidence: Grep for 'record_stable_loss_tombstones' returned line 792 as sole call site; loss_observe_mode set at line 833 with no tombstone write

component_chain.role: record_stable_loss_tombstones() — writes tombstones for removed sessions by diffing current scan against session_dirs.txt
component_chain.evidence: Read lines 359-386 directly in this session

component_chain.role: restore_online_sessions() — spawns saved sessions via daemon_spawn_session(); does NOT write session_dirs.txt after each confirmed spawn
component_chain.evidence: Read lines 1759-1765; grep for write_home_snapshot showed no call in restore_online_sessions()

component_chain.role: daemon_spawn_session() — spawns transient systemd unit; confirms daemon tracking via wait_for_daemon_tracking(); reads memory defaults from env vars (lines 1418-1420)

pre_existing_guards.purpose: Operator escape hatch to bypass tombstone checks; must remain functional
Suppresses loss observation during active restore to prevent double-counting; must not be weakened
Second restore-in-progress guard after change detection; must not be weakened

setup.reason: non-UI -- config; cycle does not produce (1) rendered UI changes, (2) browser interaction, (3) Playwright invocation, (4) screenshot evidence, or (5) any change to user-triggered code paths. This is a shell script managing session lifecycle as a background systemd service.

evidence.observed: Three structural gaps described in user requirement: tombstone writes lag 5 minutes behind loss detection; session_dirs.txt is not updated immediately after tracking confirmation; memory defaults are infinity

measured.value: record_stable_loss_tombstones() called at line 792 only (guarded by loss_stable_count >= 30 × 10s = 300s); restore_online_sessions() has no write_home_snapshot() call after daemon_spawn_session() success; lines 1418-1420 default to infinity

expected.value: Tombstone at first loss detection; session_dirs.txt update immediately after tracking confirmation; MemoryMax=4G/MemoryHigh=3G/MemorySwapMax=2G defaults
expected.source: User requirement document + server meminfo (MemTotal: 32GB, SwapTotal: 40GB)

evidence.gap: GAP 1: up to 300s tombstone lag; GAP 2: up to 60s session_dirs.txt lag; GAP 3: infinity vs. finite per-session cap

development_approach.strategy: Three surgical patches to /root/bin/happy-session-recovery.sh. GAP 1: extract removed-session set from watcher loop and write early tombstones when loss_observe_mode first becomes 1. GAP 2: add per-home confirmed-session accumulator in restore_online_sessions() and call write_home_snapshot() after each successful daemon_spawn_session(). GAP 3: change three constant defaults from infinity to 4G/3G/2G and update comment.
development_approach.validation_approach: Code review of the three patched locations. Verify (1) early tombstone is appended to session_tombstones.jsonl when nr > 0 path is entered; (2) write_home_snapshot() is called with correct session list after each successful spawn; (3) systemd-run invocation in daemon_spawn_session() shows 4G/3G/2G in the MemoryHigh/MemoryMax/MemorySwapMax properties when no env override is set. QA should grep the modified script for the changed lines and verify correctness.

research_findings.reason: All three gaps are straightforward structural defects in known code with clear fix directions. No pattern/architecture research needed — fixes reuse existing functions (write_home_snapshot, record_stable_loss_tombstones) with minor control-flow changes.
